violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Chicken was observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken was returned to oven. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
The ledge above the 3 bay sink area is in poor repair, impacting its ability to be cleaned. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Chicken was observed held at 124 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Soup, milk, meatballs, and meat pie filling observed held at 50-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Soup and meatballs observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025
Meat pie filling observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pork, and soup were placed hot in the refrigerator in wrapped containers greater than four inches deep. Employee stated the pork had been made less than 2 hours prior, and was reheated. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

A whisk observed stored on an unclean surface (on the faucet of the food prep sink) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025
The window ledge above the 3 bay sink is in poor repair, impacting its ability to be cleaned. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025

The walk in cooler is in poor repair, not holding food at 41 degrees F or above. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. No foods requiring refrigeration are to be stored in walk in cooler until it has been repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer is to be cleaned and sanitized before use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

The cutting boards in the meat area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2024
The fan cover, and the fan cover soffit in the meat area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
